title: polygala capitolensis
intro: Polygala capitolensis, also known as King's crown or Polygala, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. It belongs to the milkwort family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Polygala capitolensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide health benefits like reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting overall well-being.
